engineering.translation: When the disciplined cittam (mind) rests in the Self alone, free of longing for any desire — at that moment, and not before, the practitioner is called yukta. The verse names a condition with two clauses: cittam ātmany evāvatiṣṭhate (mind rests in the Self) and nispṛhaḥ sarva-kāmebhyaḥ (free of longing from all desires). Both must hold. Shankara reads ātmany eva as the non-dual Self; Ramanuja reads it as the self qualified-by-Brahman; both treat avatiṣṭhate as the positive resting-state, not mere absence of motion. The engineering rung the verse names operationally: the senior whose state does not depend on the next promotion, the next system-design-review approval, the next conference invitation. Coherent with 2.55 (the sthita-prajña who has withdrawn all desires) and with 5.21-23 (the contained-in-self happiness). The verse is the inner correlate of the equanimity-diagnostic in 6.7-9: the engineer whose response-shape to praise and criticism is the same is operating from a state where neither registers as the supplier of the engineer's stability. Confidence HIGH at narrow scope; the verse's full metaphysical scope (the non-dual Self) sits past the operational layer.
engineering.concrete_scenario: A staff engineer at a self-driving company has just received the response to her system-design-review for the perception-pipeline rewrite. Three principals love it. One principal — the most senior, in a different timezone — has filed a 1200-word objection that, if it lands, will probably block the design and require six weeks of rework. She reads the objection on Friday at 5 PM. Her response: she reads the objection twice, identifies the two genuine technical points and the three points that are taste-disagreement, schedules a Tuesday sync, and goes to dinner. She does not refresh the doc that evening. She does not draft the rebuttal. She does not text her manager for moral support. The block at 9 AM Saturday — a backfill design unrelated to the perception pipeline — runs cleanly. The Tuesday sync resolves into one accepted change and a polite agreement-to-disagree on the others. Compare the engineer who would have spent Friday-evening rebuttal-drafting, Saturday processing the imagined consequences, Sunday composing alternative architectures, and arrived at Tuesday's sync with three weekends of accumulated tension. The first engineer is yukta in the operational sense the verse names: her cittam was not in motion across the weekend; her state did not depend on the principal's verdict.
engineering.falsifiability: The analog fails if a reader confuses non-attachment with not-caring. The first engineer cared deeply about the perception-pipeline design — she had spent two months on it. The verse's nispṛhaḥ is not 'doesn't care'; it is 'cares but is not pushed off-state by the outcome.' An engineer who reads this verse as 'detach from work' has misread the verse and the chapter.
engineering.counter_example: When the engineer's apparent calm is in fact suppression — the principal's objection is genuinely correct and requires rethinking the design from foundations, but the engineer has trained herself into a state where critique 'rolls off' rather than landing — the verse does not endorse this. Yukta is a state of disciplined receptivity, not of insulation from feedback. The cittam that has rested in the Self is open; the cittam that has built walls is closed. The verse asks for the first.
engineering.implication: Audit which inputs to your work are currently the supplier of your stability — the manager's approval, the launch metric, the conference invitation. Where the supplier is external, the cittam is not yet at rest in the Self.
engineering.quotable_line: The yukta engineer cares deeply about the work and is not pushed off-state by the outcome.

iterations.mutation: v0 generation: BG 6.18 engineering layer drafted from triangulated source pack. Anchored on the two-clause condition (mind rests in Self + free of longing), preserved across Shankara/Ramanuja. Cross-references to 2.55 (sthita-prajña) and 5.21-23 (contained-in-self happiness) maintained per chapter-6 thesis. Engineering analog at strong-narrow scope: the senior whose state does not depend on external approvals. Counter-example explicitly distinguishes nispṛhaḥ from suppression / not-caring. Tools: GitHub, system-design-review process. Confidence HIGH at narrow scope.
